raw_input:
[3.0/5] quality control is not so good EDIT: the seller contacted me and gave me the replacement. Even though I don't like the quality, at least the seller is responsible.

I throw it away because almost every facial mask has dirty dust on it after I added water to it. Bad quality control. If you don't want your face has some unknown dust or something, then don't risk it. [4.0/5] Love these paper sheet masks, but pricier than others These are pricier than other compressed facial masks, but they have the advantage of prime shipping (as opposed to 2-3 weeks for international shipping) and of being individually wrapped. I wanted to try making my own masks asap, and also want to take these when I travel and to throw in beauty gifts (spa in a jar) for friends and family. So the fast shipping and individually wrapped masks were worth paying more for me.

I love the concept of soaking these in whatever you like, or placing over a mud mask so that my skin can soak up the benefits and detoxify without drying out too fast. However, these are a bit too small for me width-wise and definitely too small for my hubby (who is willing to sheet mask with me!). I'll probably buy these again just because I can easily travel with them and use in gifts, but I am going to look for larger ones that I can use on a more regular basis. And once you see the difference sheet masking can do for your skin, you might just look forward to using these as often as you can! I know I do!

(I found a really good recipe to brighten and moisturize the face: one egg white, teaspoon of honey, and the juice of a lemon/lime slice. Whisk it together, or shake in a jar, and dab all over your face. Then soak your mask in it and place on your face. It feels sooo good! And is cheap :) [5.0/5] as expected . as expected . [5.0/5] Great for Spa Nights at Home! I love these! They puff up in liquid like little marshmallows, and are easy to peel apart to form the full mask. It works for my face size/shape just fine (a smidge small for men maybe). They appear to be high quality, and I love that they're individually wrapped! I throw these in my travel bags to use with my serums and lotions, and give them away for people interested. I've used these with blended aloe vera, gels, serums, hyaluronic acid, high moisture "lotions" and so much more.

I usually remove the packaging, drop the "tablet" into the well-mixed up liquid/gel concoction I chose that day, and wait for it to puff up. Then I peel it apart to form the full mask, plop it back in the liquid/gel to absorb some more, and throw it in the fridge (because I like the masks cold). I use these after showering and cleaning my face, and watch tv or read with it on. Once I feel the edges start to feel slightly dry, I peel it off and massage the rest of it into my skin, and top off with another moisturizer before bed.

I liked these so much, I bought a package for my mom too! Do note, the ones I received were actually in a blue bag, with blue writing on the individual packaging. [5.0/5] Five Stars Very good! Works great. [5.0/5] a DIY option for the face mask skincare trend! If you're way into Korean skincare trends around paper masks, but both on a budget and/or have super sensitive skin, these are the answer for you!! I have been having fun concocting different DIY versions of face masks that work really well and are much more cost effective than buying the premade types. To use, make a small shot glass worth of your mask ingredients, drop the paper pellet in, let it absorb- then very carefully unfold it and put it on your face.

* Skin brightening: Mix a pinch of [[ASIN:B005VEE5YM Vitamin C Powder, USP-Grade (L-ascorbic acid) Pure - 1.8oz / 50g]] with green tea
* Detoxifying: Lemon juice, warm water, and a pinch of [[ASIN:B01G0S3Y44 Matcha Green Tea Powder Organic ( Japanese Premium Culinary Grade ) - USDA & Vegan Certified - 30g (1.06 oz) - Perfect for Baking, Smoothies, Latte, Iced Tea, Herbal Teas. Gluten & Sugar Free]]
* Healing: warm water (or milk, depending on what works for your skin), honey, and [[ASIN:B0176Q6RE8 Zint Beef Collagen Powder (1 lb): Grass-Fed Hydrolyzed Non-GMO Protein Supplement]] [4.0/5] Nice, Quality Masks. I received the masks in the blue packaging. Not sure if there's any difference than the masks in the pink. Works well my serums. Did not fall apart while in use. Will buy again. [5.0/5] Five Stars Easy to use. Like it. [5.0/5] Five Stars Good [5.0/5] Five Stars Great product, works very well for DIY masks. [4.0/5] Four Stars Really nice and absorbing. Good material. [4.0/5] Four Stars good quality, nice absort [1.0/5] One Star Very bad qualitt!!! [5.0/5] Perfect for DIY sheet masks! I love these!
I had read about doing lotion masks using cotton pads that peeled apart into layers, and bought the stuff to try it. It's good, and not hard to do, but takes a bit of time to prepare. So I bought these compressed sheet masks for doing quick lotion masks. I really like these. The compressed size makes it easy to travel with them, and they don't take up hardly any space when you store them. When you get them wet, they expand like the snake pellets you get on 4th of July. =)
I keep a shot glass in my bathroom just for doing quick lotion masks. I unwrap one of these compressed masks, toss it in the shot glass, then douse it with skin conditioner, hyaluronic acid lotion, and a bit of rose water. Once the mask is nice and saturated, I put it on my face for 3-5 minutes. (Since I like to listen to music while I'm getting ready, I leave the mask on for about the length of a song.)
The cotton material feels nice, not scratchy. When the sheet is wet it has enough stretch to cling to the face like it should. The fit is wide, the the nose flap is short. At least that's the fit on my face. I fix this by carefully cutting the excess width at my temples with scissors and placing one of strips I've cut over the end of my nose where the nose flap doesn't cover. The main thing I like about these is that the flaps on the eye holes are attached at the top. So, while I'm wearing the sheet mask, I can put the eye flaps on my eye lids so they get some moisture and still see what I'm doing. I really like that.
I plan on repurchasing these as soon as I run out. [5.0/5] Perfect for DIY sheet masks! I love these!
I had read about doing lotion masks using cotton pads that peeled apart into layers, and bought the stuff to try it. It's good, and not hard to do, but takes a bit of time to prepare. So I bought these compressed sheet masks for doing quick lotion masks. I really like these. So, while I'm wearing the sheet mask, I can put the eye flaps on my eye lids so they get some moisture and still see what I'm doing. I really like that.
I plan on repurchasing these as soon as I run out. [5.0/5] Perfect for DIY sheet masks! I love these!
I had read about doing lotion masks using cotton pads that peeled apart into layers, and bought the stuff to try it. It's good, and not hard to do, but takes a bit of time to prepare. So I bought these compressed sheet masks for doing quick lotion masks. I really like these. The compressed size makes it easy to travel with them, and they don't take up hardly any space when you store them. When you get them wet, they expand like the snake pellets you get on 4th of July. =)
I keep a shot glass in my bathroom just for doing quick lotion masks. I unwrap one of these compressed masks, toss it in the shot glass, then douse it with skin conditioner, hyaluronic acid lotion, and a bit of rose water. Once the mask is nice and saturated, I put it on my face for 3-5 minutes. (Since I like to listen to music while I'm getting ready, I leave the mask on for about the length of a song.)
The cotton material feels nice, not scratchy. When the sheet is wet it has enough stretch to cling to the face like it should. The fit is wide, the the nose flap is short. At least that's the fit on my face. I fix this by carefully cutting the excess width at my temples with scissors and placing one of strips I've cut over the end of my nose where the nose flap doesn't cover. The main thing I like about these is that the flaps on the eye holes are attached at the top. So, while I'm wearing the sheet mask, I can put the eye flaps on my eye lids so they get some moisture and still see what I'm doing. I really like that.
I plan on repurchasing these as soon as I run out. [5.0/5] Five Stars I liked it very much [5.0/5] Five Stars Amazing! [3.0/5] I like the way they are wrapped I like the way they are wrapped. It is very convenient to carry.

But some of the masks have dust on them.

I throw it away because almost every facial mask has dirty dust on it after I added water to it. Bad quality control. I wanted to try making my own masks asap, and also want to take these when I travel and to throw in beauty gifts (spa in a jar) for friends and family. So the fast shipping and individually wrapped masks were worth paying more for me.

I love the concept of soaking these in whatever you like, or placing over a mud mask so that my skin can soak up the benefits and detoxify without drying out too fast. However, these are a bit too small for me width-wise and definitely too small for my hubby (who is willing to sheet mask with me!). I'll probably buy these again just because I can easily travel with them and use in gifts, but I am going to look for larger ones that I can use on a more regular basis. And once you see the difference sheet masking can do for your skin, you might just look forward to using these as often as you can! I know I do!

To use, make a small shot glass worth of your mask ingredients, drop the paper pellet in, let it absorb- then very carefully unfold it and put it on your face.

* Skin brightening: Mix a pinch of [[ASIN:B005VEE5YM Vitamin C Powder, USP-Grade (L-ascorbic acid) Pure - 1.8oz / 50g]] with green tea
* Detoxifying: Lemon juice, warm water, and a pinch of [[ASIN:B01G0S3Y44 Matcha Green Tea Powder Organic ( Japanese Premium Culinary Grade ) - USDA & Vegan Certified - 30g (1.06 oz) - Perfect for Baking, Smoothies, Latte, Iced Tea, Herbal Teas. Gluten & Sugar Free]]

I received the masks in the blue packaging. Not sure if there's any difference than the masks in the pink. Works well my serums. Did not fall apart while in use. Will buy again. [5.0/5] Five Stars Easy to use. Like it. [5.0/5] Five Stars Good [5.0/5] Five Stars Great product, works very well for DIY masks. [4.0/5] Four Stars Really nice and absorbing. Good material. [4.0/5] Four Stars good quality, nice absort [1.0/5] One Star Very bad qualitt!!! [5.0/5] Perfect for DIY sheet masks! I love these!
I had read about doing lotion masks using cotton pads that peeled apart into layers, and bought the stuff to try it. It's good, and not hard to do, but takes a bit of time to prepare. So I bought these compressed sheet masks for doing quick lotion masks. I really like these. The compressed size makes it easy to travel with them, and they don't take up hardly any space when you store them. When you get them wet, they expand like the snake pellets you get on 4th of July. =)
I keep a shot glass in my bathroom just for doing quick lotion masks. I unwrap one of these compressed masks, toss it in the shot glass, then douse it with skin conditioner, hyaluronic acid lotion, and a bit of rose water. Once the mask is nice and saturated, I put it on my face for 3-5 minutes. (Since I like to listen to music while I'm getting ready, I leave the mask on for about the length of a song.)
The cotton material feels nice, not scratchy. When the sheet is wet it has enough stretch to cling to the face like it should. The fit is wide, the the nose flap is short. At least that's the fit on my face. I fix this by carefully cutting the excess width at my temples with scissors and placing one of strips I've cut over the end of my nose where the nose flap doesn't cover. The main thing I like about these is that the flaps on the eye holes are attached at the top. So, while I'm wearing the sheet mask, I can put the eye flaps on my eye lids so they get some moisture and still see what I'm doing. I really like that.
I plan on repurchasing these as soon as I run out. [5.0/5] Perfect for DIY sheet masks! I love these!

response:
Most customers are happy with these compressed DIY sheet masks. People love that they are individually wrapped, compact for travel, and easy to use with your own serums, lotions, or homemade recipes. The masks expand when wet, feel soft on the skin, and absorb liquids well. Several reviewers enjoy using them for spa nights at home or giving them as gifts. A few noted they run a bit small, especially for men.

The main complaints involve quality control, with a couple of reviewers finding dust or debris on the masks after adding water. One reviewer threw theirs away because of this, though they noted the seller offered a replacement. One person simply rated them very bad without much detail.

Overall, the majority of reviewers would buy these again and recommend them, particularly for people interested in Korean skincare trends or making their own custom face masks on a budget.
